title: vivo X500 Ultra may feature a 10x telephoto camera
description: The rumor mill surrounding the vivo X500 series continues to gather pace, with a fresh leak hinting at a major camera upgrade. According to the new rumor, the vivo X500 Ultra may arrive with a telephoto camera capable of delivering longer zoom reach than the vivo X300 Ultra. In a recent post on Weibo, tipster Smart Pikachu claimed that vivo is testing a 10x periscope telephoto camera for one of its upcoming flagship smartphones, which is likely to be the X500 Ultra. vivo X300 Ultra with 200mm and 400mm teleconverter lenses The tipster further revealed that the entire vivo X500...

title: Xiaomi 17T vs. 17T Pro
description: The Xiaomi 17T series is here, eight months after the 15T release and we are here to answer the same question users had before - if the Xiaomi 17T is good enough or whether the Xiaomi 17T Pro is the better choice despite its considerably higher price tag. As of now, the Xiaomi 17T and 17T Pro price gap is about €150, so it's important to consider all aspects and nuances before digging deeper into your pocket for the Pro model. You might actually be perfectly okay with the lower-end Xiaomi 17T and save a few bucks, too. On the other hand, some of you may appreciate the larger battery,...

title: Samsung Galaxy A34, A25, A16 4G, M35, and A06 are also receiving One UI 8.5 stable update
description: The Samsung Galaxy A16 5G received the One UI 8.5 stable update last week, which is now available for the Galaxy A16 4G as well. It's currently rolling out in South Korea with firmware version A165NKSU7DZE7. [#InlinePriceWidget,13346,1#] [#InlinePriceWidget,13383,1#] Additionally, Samsung has released the stable One UI 8.5 update for the Galaxy A34 and Galaxy A25 as well in South Korea with firmware versions A346NKSUEFZE6 and A256NKSU9EZE8, respectively. Samsung Galaxy A16 4G The Samsung Galaxy M35 and Galaxy A06 5G are also receiving the One UI 8.5 stable update, but in...

title: Global vivo Y500 is coming in three versions
description: Back in September of last year, vivo launched the Y500 in China. Now, the company is readying not one, not two, but three global models that will seemingly all be called Y500 too. These have the model numbers V2627, V2634, and V2635, according to the Bluetooth SIG, which just certified all of them. Unfortunately, all that this certification process reveals is that all three models do have Bluetooth support, which isn't a surprise whatsoever. Why there are three different handsets, with three different model numbers, all called Y500, though - that's a mystery that will have to live on, for...

title: Here's when Samsung is rumored to be launching the Galaxy Fit4
description: Samsung's Galaxy Fit3 fitness tracker made its debut in early 2024, so it's been quite a while since the Korean company has unveiled such a product. Fret not, however, for a new one is coming. According to a new report, Samsung is already busy working on the Galaxy Fit4. This model is expected to launch later this year. However, it will not be presented during the company's next Unpacked event, which is rumored to be taking place on July 22 in London. Instead, the Galaxy Fit4 will allegedly come a few months after that, likely around September. If so, then it will be launching...

title: iPhone Ultra foldable tipped to offer a vapor chamber
description: Months ahead of its expected debut, a fresh leak has revealed another key detail about Apple’s rumored foldable smartphone, widely believed to launch as the iPhone Ultra. According to a tipster on Weibo, the foldable phone from Apple will feature a vapor chamber for improved thermal efficiency. Vapor chamber on the iPhone 17 Pro The tipster claims that Apple has achieved ‘impressive VC cooling performance’ on the iPhone Ultra. Apple’s first foldable is rumored to feature a thin chassis, especially when unfolded. Despite the space constraints due to a slim design, Apple appears to...

title: Xiaomi joins the AirDrop party
description: The latest Android device maker to announce it's bringing support for Apple's AirDrop to Quick Share is Xiaomi. The brand has unveiled the news on X using the account for HyperOS, its Android skin. AirDrop is now available on Quick Share.Fast, seamless sharing of photos and files to Apple devices.#AirDropSupport #XiaomiHyperOS3 #Xiaomi pic.twitter.com/vqJ0w0QUbp— Xiaomi HyperOS (@XiaomiHyperOS_) June 1, 2026 As you can see, Xiaomi says AirDrop support is now available in Quick Share on its devices, but it's unclear if it's all devices or just specific ones. Of course, if you have a...

title: Realme starts teasing the P4R 5G ahead of its launch
description: Back in mid-March, the first details about the upcoming Realme P4R were leaked, and now the brand has finally started teasing the device's launch in India, through a dedicated microsite. In case you were wondering, the "P" in the P series stands for "Pioneer", and this line is "made for the bold and young" according to Realme. Unfortunately, the brand hasn't revealed any details about the P4R so far, aside from the fact that it will have 5G support. The company has, however, outed the P4R's tagline, which is "Power For Real", implying that it will have a big battery. As it stands...

title: Samsung Galaxy A54 is now receiving the update to One UI 8.5
description: Samsung is continuing its rollout of One UI 8.5, the version of its Android skin that made its debut on the Galaxy S26 family early this year. The company beta tested it until early May for older devices, and over the past few weeks it's been busy releasing it to more and more handsets. [#InlinePriceWidget,12070,1#] Today the Galaxy A54 from 2023 is the one graced with its very own taste of One UI 8.5, but with a caveat. The update is currently only available in Korea, where the Galaxy A54 is sold as the Galaxy Quantum 4. It has the same model number though, and is for all intents and...

title: Google is opening its first physical store outside the US this summer
description: Google is finally going to open its first physical store outside of the US "this summer". The company has announced that the store will be opening in Omotesando, Tokyo, Japan, specifically on the first floor of Omokado in Tokyu Plaza. The store will sell Pixel devices, Nest products, Fitbit devices, as well as accessories, and will also be offering "the latest AI experiences", whatever that means. Additionally, the store will serve as a pick-up point for orders made from Google's online store in Japan. Dedicated staff will be available to help with troubleshooting Google products....

title: Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold8 wide foldable spotted in the wild
description: Samsung is expected to launch its next foldables in July. Recent leaks suggest that the rumored wide-screen foldable could be called the Galaxy Z Fold8, while the Galaxy Z Fold7 successor may be known as the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra. Ahead of anything official, the wide-screen foldable has been spotted in real-world images. Images of the alleged wide-screen Galaxy Z Fold8 have surfaced online courtesy of a user on South Korea’s DC Inside community forum. The device appears to be wrapped in a protective anti-leak case that conceals most of its design details. However, the phone’s...

title: Apple releases iOS 26.5.1 and macOS 26.5.1
description: Today, Apple released a new iOS update, dubbed 26.5.1. As the small incrementation implies, this is anything but a big one. In fact, its sole purpose is to fix one specific bug. That said, if you have encountered this bug, it sounds like it could be pretty nasty - it can basically prevent wired charging on the iPhone Air and iPhone 17 models when the battery is nearly drained, according to the official changelog. Needless to say, when the battery is nearly drained is exactly when you most want charging to work, so it's good to see this issue fixed. Apple says only a very small number of...

title: OnePlus Turbo 6X and Turbo 6X Pro specs and images are out
description: OnePlus launched the Turbo 6 and Turbo 6V in January, and now the company is rumored to be working on two more members to add to this family: the Turbo 6X and Turbo 6X Pro. Both of these have now been spotted in the Google Play Console, following their prior certifications in China by TENAA. According to those, the Turbo 6X has a 6.72-inch FHD+ LCD screen with 144Hz refresh rate, the Dimensity 7400 SoC at the helm, 6/8/12GB of RAM, 128/256/512GB of storage, and a 7,000 mAh battery. On the rear is a 50MP main camera with a 2MP decorative sensor next to it, while for selfies you get an 8MP...

title: Photo of the iPhone Fold (in white/silver) shared by Ice Universe
description: Alright, show of hands – who here thought that Apple’s first foldable will be a wide-style device, instead of the more common book-style or flip? Well, that is what Cupertino went for as you can see in the photo shared by Ice Universe on Weibo. This doesn’t come out of the blue – we’ve seen dummies with this design and CAD schematics that revealed the dimensions – 120.6 mm tall and 167.6mm wide (unfolded). When folded, the device is expected to measure 9.23mm (well, 13mm with the camera island). An alleged photo of the Apple iPhone foldable This photo also shows off one of the...

title: AMD rehashes old products at Computex 2026 with 5800X3D, 7700X3D, and RX 9070 GRE
description: AMD today announced three products at Computex 2026. One of these is a re-released 4-year-old CPU, another is a scaled down version of a 3-year-old CPU, and the third is a year-old graphics card. Just in case you were wondering how the home PC gaming industry is currently going. The first is the Ryzen 7 5800X3D, which was discontinued in 2024 after a two-year run. This was the first CPU by AMD to feature the company's 3D V-Cache technology. It's now being brought back, as it is based on the AM4 platform that can still be run on DDR4 memory. Speaking of AM4, the platform is...

title: Nvidia claims its new Vera ARM CPU is 80% faster than leading x86 CPUs
description: Nvidia just announced the RTX Spark – this is AI server tech trickling down to the consumer space with a Grace CPU (20 cores), a Blackwell GPU (6,144 CUDA cores) and 128GB of LPDDR5X. Now here’s what’s next for servers – and maybe one day consumer devices too. The new Vera CPU is the CPU half of the Vera Rubin platform – the other being the Rubin GPU. Vera promises a 1.8x average speedup over “leading x86 CPUs” (Nvidia didn’t name them explicitly). Vera is massive – it has 88 Olympus cores (based on the ARM instruction set) with Spatial Multithreading for 176 threads per socket. The...

title: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra with Nvidia's RTX Spark is coming this fall
description: Microsoft's next Surface will be its most powerful yet. The Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra was just announced at Computex 2026, and it's taking the MacBook Pro head-on. The Surface Laptop Ultra packs the just-announced Nvidia RTX Spark system on a chip. It packages up to 20 ARM CPU cores, up to 128GB of LPDDR5X unified RAM, and a Blackwell GPU with 6,144 CUDA cores, which Microsoft says roughly translates to an Nvidia RTX 5070. The power draw is expected to reach up to 80W, so a low-powered 5070. Microsoft says the thermal headroom of the new Surface Laptop Ultra is 2.5x that of the...

title: Asus ROG Xbox Ally X20 unveiled with larger 7.4" OLED screen, new TMR joysticks
description: The Steam Deck used to be the go-to x86 gaming handheld, but after the recent ridiculous price hike, it’s hard to recommend. There are alternatives like the Asus ROG Xbox Ally X, which launched last year at $1,000 (24GB/1TB). This used to be a lot of money, except the 16GB/1TB OLED Deck is $950 now. In our review of the Xbox Ally X, we pointed out some major cons for such an expensive handheld, e.g. having an LCD instead of an OLED panel and the iffy analog sticks. We have some good news and some bad news – the new Asus ROG Xbox Ally X20 bundle solves both issues. Wait, a bundle? Well,...

title: A photo story: I took the Find X9 Ultra to the UCL final in Budapest
description: The past weekend I had the incredible opportunity to visit the beautiful city of Budapest. Better yet, I was able to attend the biggest game in this season's club football – the UEFA Champions League Final, featuring French side Paris Saint Germain from France and England's Arsenal. Oppo, which is an official global partner of UEFA for the tournament were kind enough to offer me a ticket for the game so I figured I'd use the opportunity to test the Find X9 Ultra's camera and the teleconverter from its Earth Explorer Kit and how well it can serve on my travels. Now don't expect any...

title: Xiaomi launches a new Bold Yellow color for the Poco X8 Pro
description: Xiaomi introduced a new paintjob in an attempt to refresh one of its mid-rangers and put it back in the spotlight. The hue is called Bold Yellow color and it's put on top of the Poco X8 Pro. Poco X8 Pro in Bold Yellow The company says the new Bold Yellow color is inspired by the racetrack with all the stripes and the black accent color around the frame. We don't get to see an accented frame that often, and it looks nice, at least in those official images. However, the back panel has a more industrial vibe to it with the bolts and the semi-transparent-like design. Of...
